title: Interpret motion graphs
description: Interpret graphs that describe motion along a line.
topic_path: Straight-line motion: connecting position, velocity, and acceleration

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is moving along a line. The following graph gives the object's velocity over time. 

**For each point on the graph, is the object moving forward, backward, or neither?**

[[☃ label-image 1]]
hints:
The direction of movement is indicated by the velocity's sign:

- If the velocity is positive, the object is moving forward.
- If the velocity is negative, the object is moving backward.
- If the velocity is zero, the object is not moving.

These are the correct selections:

Point | Direction
:- | -:
First (leftmost) | Moving forward
Second | Moving backward
Third | Neither
Fourth (rightmost) | Moving forward

options.imageAlt: A graph with time in seconds on the horizontal axis and velocity, in meters per second, on the vertical axis has a periodic graph that begins at the origin, reaches a maximum in quadrant 1, then a minimum in quadrant 4, and a maximum in quadrant 1.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is at a local maximum and positive
Second point, where graph is decreasing and negative
Third point, where graph is increasing and crosses the x-axis
Fourth point, where graph is increasing and positive

options.imageAlt: A graph with time, in seconds, on the horizontal axis and velocity, in meters per second, on the vertical axis has a decreasing graph that begins concave down in quadrant 1 at t equals 0 and becomes concave up shortly before it crosses the x-axis into quadrant 4. There are 4 points marked from left to right on the graph.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is decreasing and positive
Second point, where graph is decreasing and positive
Third point, where graph is decreasing and negative
Fourth point, where graph is decreasing and negative

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is increasing and negative
Second point, where graph is increasing and intersects the x-axis
Third point, where graph is increasing and positive

There are 4 points marked from left to right on the graph.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is decreasing and intersects the x-axis
Second point, where graph is at a local minimum and negative
Third point, where graph is at a local maximum and positive
Fourth point, where graph is increasing and negative

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is moving along a line. The following graph gives the object's position, relative to its starting point, over time.

**For each point on the graph, is the object moving forward, backward, or neither?**

[[☃ label-image 1]]
hints:
The direction of the position graph indicates the direction of movement:

- If the graph is increasing, the object is moving forward.
- If the graph is decreasing, the object is moving backward.
- If the graph is neither increasing not decreasing, the object is not moving. This happens at extremum points of the graph.

These are the correct selections, from left to right:

Point | Direction
:- | -:
First (leftmost) | Moving forward
Second | Moving forward
Third | Moving forward
Fourth (rightmost) | Moving forward

options.imageAlt: A graph with time, in seconds, on the horizontal axis and position, in meters, on the vertical axis has an increasing graph that begins concave up in quadrant 4 at t equals 0 and becomes concave down when it crosses the x-axis into quadrant 1. There are 4 points marked from left to right on the graph.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is increasing and negative
Second point, where graph is increasing and crosses the x-axis
Third point, where graph is increasing and positive
Fourth point, where graph is increasing and positive

:- | -:

options.imageAlt: A graph with time, in seconds, on the horizontal axis and position, in meters, on the vertical axis has a periodic graph that begins in quadrant 1 at t equals 0, reaches a minimum in quadrant 4, then a maximum in quadrant 1, then decreases into quadrant 4. There are 4 points marked from left to right on the graph.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is decreasing and positive
Second point, where graph is at a local minimum and negative
Third point, where graph is increasing and negative
Fourth point, where graph is at a local maximum and positive

**For each point on the graph, is the object moving forward, backward, or neither?**

[[☃ label-image 1]]
hints:
The direction of the position graph indicates the direction of movement:

- If the graph is increasing, the object is moving forward.
- If the graph is decreasing, the object is moving backward.
- If the graph is neither increasing nor decreasing, the object is not moving. This happens at extremum points of the graph.

These are the correct selections, from left to right:

Point | Direction
:- | -:
First (leftmost) | Moving backward
Second | Neither

options.imageAlt: A graph with time, in seconds, on the horizontal axis and position, in meters, on the vertical axis has a graph that begins in quadrant 4 at t equals 0, reaches a local maximum in quadrant 1, then a local minimum, a local maximum, and another local minimum in quadrant 4, then a local maximum in quadrant 1 and a local minimum in quadrant 4. There are 4 points marked from left to right on the graph.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is decreasing and positive
Second point, where graph is at a local maximum and negative
Third point, where graph is increasing and positive
Fourth point, where graph is decreasing and negative

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is moving along a line. The following graph gives the object's velocity over time.

**For each point on the graph, is the object speeding up, slowing down, or neither?**

[[☃ label-image 1]]
hints:
Speed is the magnitude of velocity. When analyzing linear motion, speed is the same as the absolute value of velocity.

So the object is speeding up whenever the absolute value of the velocity is increasing, and slowing down whenever the absolute value of the velocity is decreasing.

In summary, let's consider the sign and direction of velocity:

Sign / Direction | Increasing | Decreasing
:- | :-: | -:
Positive | Speed up | Slow down
Negative | Slow down | Speed up

When the velocity is zero, the object isn't speeding up or slowing down.

Furthermore, when the velocity is neither increasing nor decreasing, the object is neither speeding up or slowing down. This happens at extremum points of the velocity graph.

These are the correct selections:

Point | Change in speed
:- | -:
First (leftmost) | Speeding up
Second | Slowing down
Third | Neither
Fourth (rightmost) | Speeding up

options.imageAlt: A graph with time in seconds on the horizontal axis and velocity, in meters per second, on the vertical axis has a concave down graph that begins at t equals 0 and v equals 3, gradually increases in quadrant 1, then decreases until it is in quadrant 4.

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is increasing and positive
Second point, where graph is decreasing and positive
Third point, where graph is decreasing and intercepts the x-axis
Fourth point, where graph is decreasing and negative

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is increasing and positive

Third point, where graph is decreasing and positive

markers.label: Leftmost point, where graph is decreasing and positive
